headlines.title: EU's Russian LNG Imports Hit Record High Ahead of 2027 Ban
headlines.summary: The European Union imported a record 9.97 million metric tons of liquefied natural gas (LNG) worth €5.96 billion (~$6.82 billion) from Russia's Yamal LNG facility in the first half of 2026, marking a 16% increase compared to the same period in 2025 as they front-loaded Russian en

headlines.title: Why Renewed Iran Tensions Could Keep Fuel Prices Elevated
headlines.summary: The oil market has officially entered its mixed-signals era. Crude prices had erased most of their wartime gains as barrels returned to the market and fears of oversupply resurfaced. But renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities have sent prices climbing again, underscoring just how quickly

headlines.title: Global Emissions Hit Another Record High Despite Clean Energy Boom
headlines.summary: The Energy Institute has released the 2026 Statistical Review of World Energy, published in partnership with Ember and in collaboration with KPMG and Kearney. headlines.title: Nigeria’s Oil Production Hits Six-Year High as Middle East Risks Return
headlines.summary: Nigeria’s crude oil production hit a six-year high in June, as operations continued to stabilize and no major outage incidents occurred, just as the new flare-up in Iran threatens flows through the Strait of Hormuz again. Nigeria pumped 1.56 million barrels per day (bpd) of crude

headlines.title: Iran Warns U.S. Interference Could Trigger Further Oil and Gas Disruptions
headlines.summary: Following a major U.S.-Iran re-escalation over the Strait of Hormuz this weekend, Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) on Monday warned that continued U.S. interference in the chokepoint “could lead to greater incidents in the global oil and gas sector.” The renewed ho

headlines.title: European Natural Gas Prices Jump on Hormuz Escalation
headlines.summary: Europe’s benchmark natural gas prices opened 3% higher on Monday following the weekend escalation over the Strait of Hormuz which threatens LNG shipments out of the Middle East again. headlines.title: Oil and LNG Tankers Go Dark Again as Hormuz Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: Oil and LNG tankers are back to switching off transponders when transiting the Strait of Hormuz as the latest escalation in the region and last week’s Iranian attacks on commercial ships prompted vessel operators to brave the chokepoint unobserved. Total tanker traffic collapsed

headlines.title: Oil Prices Surge 4% as U.S.-Iran Escalation Rekindles Supply Fears
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped by 4% in Asian trade on Monday, following a weekend of intense U.S. strikes on Iran, claims from Iran that the Strait of Hormuz is closed to traffic once again, and Iranian strikes on five U.S. allies in the region. Brent Crude futures surged by 4% to top $79 pe

headlines.title: Big Oil’s War-Related Profits Anger Governments
headlines.summary: Supermajors are set to report bumper profits for the second quarter thanks to the surge in oil and gas prices, driven higher by the hostilities between the United States, Israel, and Iran. headlines.title: Uruguay’s Offshore Oil Province Could Be Bigger Than Vaca Muerta
headlines.summary: Companies are gearing up for exploration drilling in deepwater blocks offshore Uruguay in the hope that the South Atlantic margin offshore South America could hold a lot of oil, similar to the recent discoveries offshore Namibia in Africa.
